summary: This page explains how linearity and superposition simplify the analysis of linear systems by allowing outputs to be decomposed into zero-input and zero-state components and by expressing responses to composite inputs as sums of responses to simpler inputs. It illustrates that arbitrary signals can be approximated as weighted sums of impulses or step functions, so knowing the system response to these basic components allows determination of the response to general inputs. The page also foreshadows later use of sinusoids and exponentials as fundamental signal components.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.27 Signal representation in terms of impulse and step components.
